description: A designed landscape social-media graphic in DHS/CBP house style. The right two-thirds shows a color photograph of a female CBP officer seated in what appears to be a vehicle inspection booth or patrol vehicle; she wears a dark 'CBP' embroidered ball cap, a black tactical/police jacket with a 'POLICE OFFICER' nameplate (name 'V. Rodriguez' partially visible) and a CBP officer badge, and she is speaking into a shoulder radio mic. The left two-thirds is overlaid with a large semi-transparent green block design: the letters 'CBP' appear in enormous bold green capitals across the top. A solid green horizontal banner in the lower-left carries white bold text reading 'Thank you to our CBP workforce for safeguarding our borders and securing lawful trade and travel.' Below that, in black bold text, is the hashtag '#5DaysofThanks.' The circular blue U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal sits in the bottom-right corner. Visual style: government-branded gratitude/promotional card combining documentary photography with flat graphic overlays in CBP green and DHS blue. The messaging technique is workforce valorization and institutional self-praise — pairing a holiday-season 'thanks' campaign with the agency's enforcement mission (border security, lawful trade/travel). Apparent intent: build public goodwill toward CBP and its officers, targeting the general public and the agency's own workforce.
notable_text: CBP | Thank you to our CBP workforce for safeguarding our borders and securing lawful trade and travel. | #5DaysofThanks
